key_file_excerpts.composer.json:
{
  "name": "smartresponsor/accessing",
  "description": "Symfony 8 account access application for registration, verification, MFA, recovery, sessions, and security events.",
  "type": "project",
  "license": "proprietary",
  "require": {
    "php": "^8.4",
    "ext-ctype": "*",
    "ext-iconv": "*",
    "doctrine/doctrine-bundle": "3.1",
    "doctrine/doctrine-migrations-bundle": "3.6",
    "doctrine/orm": "^3.3",
    "scheb/2fa-bundle": "8.5",
    "scheb/2fa-totp": "8.5",
    "symfony/console": "8.0.*",
    "symfony/dotenv": "8.0.*",
    "symfony/form": "8.0.*",
    "symfony/framework-bundle": "8.0.*",
    "symfony/mailer": "8.0.*",
    "symfony/monolog-bundle": "^3.10",
    "symfony/notifier": "8.0.*",
    "symfony/password-hasher": "8.0.*",
    "symfony/rate-limiter": "8.0.*",
    "symfony/runtime": "8.0.*",
    "symfony/security-bundle": "8.0.*",
    "symfony/security-csrf": "8.0.*",
    "symfony/serializer": "8.0.*",
    "symfony/twig-bundle": "8.0.*",
    "symfony/translation": "8.0.*",
    "symfony/validator": "8.0.*",
    "symfony/yaml": "8.0.*",
    "symfonycasts/reset-password-bundle": "^1.23",
    "symfonycasts/verify-email-bundle": "^1.17",
    "twig/extra-bundle": "^3.15",
    "twig/twig": "^3.15"
  },
  "require-dev": {
    "doctrine/doctrine-fixtures-bundle": "^4.1",
    "fakerphp/faker": "^1.24",
    "friendsofphp/php-cs-fixer": "3.94",
    "phpmd/phpmd": "^2.15",
    "phpstan/phpstan": "^2.1",
    "symfony/browser-kit": "8.0.*",
    "symfony/css-selector": "8.0.*",
    "symfony/maker-bundle": "^1.64",
    "symfony/panther": "^2.2",
    "symfony/phpunit-bridge": "^7.3"
  },
  "autoload": {
    "psr-4": {
      "App\\": "src/"
    }
  },
  "autoload-dev": {
    "psr-4": {
      "App\\Tests\\": "tests/"
    }
  },
  "scripts": {
    "lint": "php -l $(git ls-files '*.php')",
    "cs:check": "php-cs-fixer fix --dry-run --diff --verbose",
    "cs:fix": "php-cs-fixer fix --verbose",
    "stan": "phpstan analyse",
    "test": "vendor/bin/simple-phpunit",
    "pipeline:local:full": [
      "@lint",
      "@cs:check",
      "@stan",
      "@test"
    ]
  },
  "extra": {
    "public-dir": "public",
    "product-name": "Accessing",
    "core-entity": "Account"
  },
  "minimum-stability": "stable",
  "prefer-stable": true,
  "config": {
    "allow-plugins": {
      "symfony/runtime": true
    }
  }
}

verdict.summary: Symfony 8 account-access application with clear scope, strong security features, and comprehensive tests; main gaps are CI/CD automation and Antora-linked docs.
strengths: Clear product scope and manifests in repo
Solid Symfony 8 + Doctrine 3 architecture with domain-oriented structure
Comprehensive tests (unit, integration/functional, Panther, Playwright) and static analysis tools
Strong security posture (2FA/TOTP, CSRF, password hashing, rate limiting)
risks: No CI/CD workflows present in the repo
Repository docs not wired into Antora report path
PHP ^8.4 requirement tightens deployment targets without automated checks
next_actions: Add GitHub Actions workflow to run lint, CS tools, PHPStan, PHPUnit, Panther, and Playwright on push/PR
Publish/attach Antora module content for the component report path
Document/run the local pipeline in CI (composer scripts and Playwright) with artifacts
Add basic metrics/tracing instrumentation and document operational runbooks
Validate production environment against PHP ^8.4 requirement in automated checks
notes: Assessment grounded in repository tree and manifests at commit bb0013d.
Security features and testing breadth are evidenced by dependencies and test directories; delivery automation is not present in-repo.

product.summary: Product identity and scope are explicit via README and PRODUCT_MANIFEST, describing registration, verification, MFA, recovery, sessions, and security events.
risks: Docs are not integrated into the Antora report path for portfolio visibility
Proprietary license may limit external adoption paths
improvements: Publish/align the Antora component page with existing repo manifests
Extend README with high-level user journeys and entry points

architecture.summary: Symfony 8 + Doctrine ORM 3 with a layered structure (Entities/Repositories/Services) and domain/architecture manifests; database migrations are present.
risks: Architecture/documentation drift risk without CI enforcement
No explicit service boundaries or deployment topology evidenced in-repo
improvements: Add static analysis and architectural checks to CI to prevent drift
Keep BOUNDING_MANIFEST and ARCHITECTURE_MANIFEST referenced from the Antora page

runtime.summary: Runtime shape is material: Symfony front controller and Kernel, env management via .env files, Monolog and rate limiting configured, and 2FA integration present.
risks: Production readiness constraints if hosting does not meet PHP ^8.4 requirement
Reliance on .env files suggests the need to harden environment configuration for production
improvements: Validate production environment compatibility with PHP ^8.4 in automated checks
Document production environment variables and secret sources in repo docs

qa.summary: Broad QA posture with unit/integration/functional tests, Panther and Playwright E2E, plus PHPStan, PHPMD, and CS fixer wired via composer scripts.
risks: No CI workflow to enforce the local pipeline or run E2E in headless mode
Test coverage and thresholds are not documented in-repo
improvements: Add a GitHub Actions workflow running composer pipeline and Playwright with artifacts
Publish minimal coverage reporting and thresholds in CI

ops.summary: Security and basic logging are strong (2FA/TOTP, CSRF, password hashing, rate limiting, Monolog), but delivery automation and observability depth are not evidenced.
risks: No GitHub Actions workflows for CI/CD in the repo
improvements: Introduce CI/CD workflow to run tests and checks on push/PR
Add metrics/tracing instrumentation and document operational procedures

market.summary: Positioning is clear for an account access service within the ecosystem; manifests and README communicate scope and core entity (Account).
risks: Proprietary license narrows external ecosystem engagement
Absence of Antora-linked docs may reduce portfolio visibility
improvements: Expose key value propositions and integration points on the Antora component page
Reference security features (MFA, recovery flows) prominently in docs

overall.summary: Strong foundation with security and testing depth; primary RC blockers are lack of CI/CD automation and Antora-linked documentation.
risks: Delivery automation gap could slow feedback and regressions detection
improvements: Add end-to-end CI pipeline and publish Antora docs to raise RC readiness
